question: 1. What is the time complexity of the operation to remove the root from a heap?

explanations.a: Correct. Removing the root itself is an O(1) operation, but restoring the heap property (heapify) takes O(log n). This question only asks about the removal step.
explanations.b: Incorrect. O(n) is not the time complexity for removing the root in a heap.
explanations.c: Incorrect. O(log n) is the time complexity for restoring the heap property after removal, not for the removal itself.
explanations.d: Incorrect. There is enough information to determine the time complexity.

question: 2. How many operations are required to remove the root if there are 10 elements in a heap?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Only one removal does not account for all elements; each removal reduces the heap size by one.
explanations.b: Incorrect. There are 10 elements, so 10 removals are needed to remove all roots.
explanations.c: Correct. To remove all elements (roots) from a heap of 10 elements, you need 10 remove operations.
explanations.d: Incorrect. 20 is not the correct number of operations for this scenario.
